label: Showcase Bold
description: Image-forward portfolio variant with stronger attitude and project contrast.
signatureMotif: image-led project framing, strong contrast, and curated metadata overlays
promptHints: Lead with project imagery and work identity before biography copy.
Every project block should feel curated and memorable, not like a feature card.

layouts: Use a cinematic hero with one dominant project image, a compact intro block, and stacked client-year-role metadata pinned beside it.
Build the work index as alternating full-width project bands with oversized imagery, short titles, and overlay metadata anchored to image edges.
Compose featured case studies with a 70/30 split: immersive visual gallery first, then a narrow sticky column for project context and credits.
Arrange gallery pages as irregular masonry rows with occasional extra-tall images, letting select projects break the grid for deliberate emphasis.
Use a minimal about/contact page with a bold portrait or studio image on one side and tight, high-contrast biography details on the other.
motifs: Dark charcoal surfaces with near-white type, one warm primary accent, and occasional green accent notes against image-heavy compositions.
Large rounded image frames paired with compact uppercase metadata chips and small overlay labels sitting directly on project photography.
Bricolage Grotesque headlines feel oversized and assertive, while DM Sans metadata stays tight, crisp, and editorial around the visuals.
Project cards should read like curated posters: sparse copy, strong edge contrast, and layered captions hovering over imagery.
antiPatterns: Avoid dense multi-column text sections that compete with project imagery; this variant should reveal work first and explanation second.
Never turn projects into generic SaaS-style feature cards with icons, buttons, and equal-height boxes that flatten visual hierarchy.
Avoid soft pastel surfaces or airy white spacing systems; this style needs dark contrast, weight, and gallery-like drama.
Do not lead with a long personal biography hero; open with memorable work imagery and let identity emerge through project framing.
